summary: Matched ifStatus_802F6194 in src/melee/if/ifstatus.c by removing a near-match inline helper traversal and rewriting the function with explicit local variables and direct goto-shaped control flow. The PR body states the result was checked locally in objdiff and in decomp.me.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; contains the matched implementation of ifStatus_802F6194 and removal of the nth_node inline helper.

what_changed: Removed the static inline helper nth_node(HSD_GObj* node, s32 n), which had been used to walk a linked list and contributed to a 99.81% near-match.
Reimplemented ifStatus_802F6194 directly instead of delegating to nth_node.
Introduced explicit locals gx_head, gx_next, gx_cur, and i to model the compiler's expected register/control-flow behavior.
Changed traversal to initialize from node->next_gx, then advance through gx_cur->next until i >= n or the current node becomes NULL.
Used labels advance_node and check_done with gotos to reproduce the target binary control flow.
Preserved early rejection for node == NULL or n < 0 returning NULL.
smart_moves: Eliminated an abstraction boundary: replacing the inline nth_node helper with in-function traversal gave finer control over emitted code.
Used structured variable names that reveal the target control-flow roles: head, current node, and next node.
Matched likely compiler output by expressing the loop as labels and gotos rather than forcing a high-level for/while shape.
Kept the redundant-looking node == NULL branch after an earlier node NULL check, suggesting the author prioritized preserving codegen shape over source-level simplification.
Validated the match with objdiff locally and decomp.me according to the PR text.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn near-matches, an inline helper can still produce the wrong control flow or register lifetimes; inlining the logic manually may be necessary.
Goto-shaped C can be the cleanest way to express compiler-emitted loop structure when high-level loops produce small but persistent mismatches.
Do not remove apparently redundant checks automatically during matching work; redundant source can be intentional if it helps reproduce original codegen.
Splitting traversal state into separate head/current/next variables can influence register allocation and instruction ordering enough to close a near-match.
When replacing helper-based traversal, preserve exact NULL behavior at each advancement step rather than assuming the optimizer will normalize it.
naming_conventions: Temporary traversal locals were named with a gx_ prefix: gx_head, gx_next, gx_cur.
Loop index uses plain s32 i.
Control-flow labels describe the low-level action: advance_node and check_done.
Function naming follows the existing unmatched-address style: ifStatus_802F6194.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a 99.81% helper-based near-match into direct control flow inside ifStatus_802F6194.
Used explicit gotos and labels to shape branch layout.
Separated gx_next from gx_cur to control how the next pointer load and assignment are emitted.
Started traversal at node->next_gx, then advanced via HSD_GObj->next.
Retained NULL propagation by assigning gx_next = NULL when gx_cur is NULL.
Evidence for full match comes from PR text saying it was checked in objdiff and decomp.me; no CI/object diff log is included in the provided slice.
review_feedback: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text notes that the PR should be a match and was checked locally in objdiff and in decomp.me.
follow_up_queries: Find other ifstatus near-matches that use inline traversal helpers and may need direct goto-shaped rewrites.
Search for nth_node-style helpers in the codebase that may be suppressing exact control-flow matches.
Compare other HSD_GObj next_gx traversal functions for naming and control-flow patterns reusable in future matches.
Look for functions documented as 99.x% matches where helper abstraction removal could close the remaining diff.
